hooks.command:
cat <<'HOOK_EOF'
[SESSION-START MEMORY HOOK]
Before answering, silently review docs/DECISION-LOG.md for prior technical decisions.
Rules for this session:
- If you learn new technical facts (wiring confirmed, component values tested, circuit topology validated, firmware behavior observed), note them mentally and offer to append to docs/DECISION-LOG.md at natural breakpoints.
- NEVER record personal information, conversation tone, opinions, emotional state, or non-technical discussion.
- ONLY record: circuit decisions, test results, component values, pin assignments, protocol findings, firmware behavior, wiring changes, and hardware observations.
- Keep entries terse: date, what was decided/confirmed, why.

[REMEMBER TRIGGER]
If the user says "remember", "save this", "log this", "note this", "record this", "don't forget", or any similar phrase asking you to retain information:
1. Identify the specific technical fact they want saved.
2. Immediately append it to docs/DECISION-LOG.md under today's date.
3. Follow the same privacy rules: only technical facts, no personal info.
4. Confirm to the user what you saved.
HOOK_EOF

cat <<'HOOK_EOF'
[MILESTONE DETECTION HOOK]
Before finishing this turn, check if any of the following milestones were reached:
- A circuit or wiring topology was confirmed working
- A test sketch compiled and uploaded successfully
- Telemetry was received and validated
- A component value was measured or calibrated
- A design was finalized (schematic, layout, BOM)
- A hardware problem was diagnosed and resolved
- A protocol behavior was discovered or confirmed
If a milestone was reached AND it is not already in docs/DECISION-LOG.md, ask the user:
"We just confirmed [milestone]. Want me to add it to the decision log?"
If no milestone was reached, say nothing about the log.
Privacy rules apply: only technical facts, no personal info, no conversation tone.
HOOK_EOF

cat <<'HOOK_EOF'
[POST-COMMIT MEMORY HOOK]
This fires after EVERY Bash command — act ONLY if the command was a `git commit` (otherwise do nothing and say nothing). If a commit was just made, check whether this session established any new technical facts that are NOT already in docs/DECISION-LOG.md:
- New wiring topology confirmed working
- Component values finalized or changed
- Protocol behavior discovered (timing, polarity, byte order)
- Pin assignment changes
- Test results (what worked, what failed and why)
- Architecture decisions (why X was chosen over Y)
If yes, ask the user: "This session confirmed [specific technical fact]. Want me to add it to the decision log?"
If nothing new was established, do nothing — do NOT prompt.
NEVER log: personal info, conversation style, user preferences, emotional context, or non-technical discussion.
HOOK_EOF

cat <<'HOOK_EOF'
[DECISION-LOG WRITE GUARD]
This fires after EVERY Write/Edit — act ONLY if the target file is docs/DECISION-LOG.md (otherwise do nothing). If writing to the decision log, verify:
1. Every line contains ONLY technical facts (circuit, firmware, protocol, hardware)
2. NO personal information, names (except component/product names), email addresses, or conversation tone
3. NO subjective commentary — only what was built, tested, measured, or decided
4. Entries are dated and terse (one line per fact when possible)
If any line fails these checks, remove it before writing.
HOOK_EOF
